tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, potassium, and significant proteinuria, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, high protein, glucose, bilirubin, and ketones) indicate possible nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury. Low HbA1c excludes chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying renal pathology.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
